alkylation reagent: alkylation (15 mM 2-chloroacetamide, for 30 min at room temperature)
alkylation concentration: alkylation (15 mM 2-chloroacetamide, for 30 min at room temperature)
cleavage agent: overnight (~ 16 h) digestion with trypsin/Lys-C mix (1:25 protease: protein; Promega)
collision energy: fragmented by collision induced dissociation in ion trap (NCE 30%; normalized AGC target of 100%; max IT 35 ms)
enrichment method: devices coated (functionalized) with Annexin-V exhibited higher capture efficiency
fractionation method: an offline fractionation of the combined sample (~ 200 μg) into 8 fractions was performed using high pH reversed-phase peptide fractionation kit
fragmentation method: the top 10 precursors from each MS2 were fragmented by HCD followed by Orbitrap analysis
instrument: Samples were then run on the Orbitrap Ascend with FAIMSpro
labeling: labeling was performed by transferring the entire digest to TMT reagent vial
reduction reagent: upon reduction (5 mM DTT, for 30 min at 45 °C)
reduction concentration: upon reduction (5 mM DTT, for 30 min at 45 °C)
